title: ByteAsk brings debugger-first verification to C and C++ coding agents
friendlyTitle: ByteAsk checks C and C++ code with real developer tools
summary: ByteAsk launched a C/C++-focused coding agent that edits repositories from the terminal and verifies changes with compilers, test suites, sanitizers, perf tooling, and live debugger sessions such as gdb. Its pitch is that C and C++ need proof through the real toolchain rather than plausible patches.
friendlySummary: ByteAsk is an AI coding tool that writes changes, then runs compilers, tests, sanitizers, and debuggers before showing the diff.
whyItMatters: Coding agents are most valuable when they can reduce review burden without increasing risk. For systems languages, grounding fixes in builds, sanitizers, and debugger state is a practical pattern other coding tools may need to copy.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This is useful because C and C++ bugs can be serious. AI code should be tested by real tools, not just sound convincing.
